core_behavior.note: All three models agree on the fundamental read-through caching behavior: check cache first, return cached response on hit, fetch and store on miss.

inputs.note: Model A specifies detailed HTTP method filtering, cache-control header honoring, Vary header support, and SHA-256 key hashing; Model B lists all HTTP methods as cacheable inputs; Model C restricts to GET only — these are independent design choices not constrained by the requirement.

outputs.note: Model A specifies detailed cache-diagnostic headers (X-Cache, X-Cache-Age, Age) and bypass reasons; Models B and C specify only basic hit/miss response data and optional metrics/status indicators without header-level detail.

assumptions.note: Model A defaults TTL to 300 seconds (5 minutes), Model B defaults to 1 hour, and Model C defaults to 5 minutes — models agree a default TTL is needed but specify different values; all three models omit explicit cache storage location and invalidation strategy assumptions.

failure_modes.note: Model A has an empty failure_modes array while Models B and C both specify fallback-to-API behavior and cache-full eviction handling.

edge_cases.note: Model A has an empty edge_cases array while Models B and C populate it with distinct edge cases such as cache key collisions, large responses, and concurrent requests.

security_constraints.note: Model A has an empty security_constraints array; Model B specifies encryption of cached data and access controls; Model C specifies not caching sensitive data and respecting cache-control headers — Models B and C introduce different security assumptions not present in Model A.

performance_constraints.note: Model A has an empty performance_constraints array; Model B specifies numeric targets (80% hit ratio, 100 req/s, <50ms latency); Model C specifies qualitative goals (cache retrieval significantly faster than API, high volume handling) — independently introduced design choices.

summary: ASM (Assumption Divergence) was the most frequently assigned category, appearing in 3 of the 8 fields.
